User and CO authentication via SSH or consol. Minimum of 10 ASCII character passwords.
Timed access mechanism allows max of 10 attempts / min. Probability of guessing: 10/(96^10) < 1/100,000.
Strength of signature algorithm, minimum 112- bits. Probability of success for random attempt: 1/(2^112) < 1/1,000,000.
A rate of 1 CPU cycle per failed authentication for the ARM-cortex A72 processor (2.2 GHz) allows for the probability of success by brute- force attack: 60 x 2.2 x 10^9 x 1/(2^112) < 1/100,000.

On demand execution of all pre-operational and conditional algorithm self-tests
This command erases all data, including all configuration information, returning the module to its factory default state The system is then rebooted.
This command erases all keys and CSPS from storage. The forced power cycle also zeroizes SSPs in volatile memory.
Yes, CO via invocation of zeroize CLI command.
Zeroization of SSPs in RAM via invocation of local or remote reset service.
RAM is volatile and all data is lost when power is taken off. Zeroization is practically instantaneous.
Yes, both User and CO, via invocation of Local Reset or Remote Reset services.
Zeroization of SSPs in memory when no longer needed.
Use of explicit zeroization function destroys SSP information immediately by overwriting memory area with zeroes.
No. The operator cannot directly initiate this method.

Checks that any file that is executed is registered in a manifest of executable files that comes with the firmware. Test verifies the integrity of the operational environment is being enforced by having the kernel attempt to run a specific executable file that does not contain a hash in the manifest file, verifying it cannot be executed.
